symptoms.symptom_name: Well-demarcated itchy scaly groin plaque with a raised arcuate advancing scaly border and central clearing, slowly spreading onto the inner thighs over ~3 weeks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A well-demarcated plaque with a raised, arcuate, actively-advancing scaly margin and central clearing is the characteristic morphology of a groin dermatophyte infection; described and video-observable, though KOH/culture is confirmatory.
Flexural psoriasis is typically a smooth, shiny, moist, NON-scaly plaque without an advancing scaly border or central clearing — the prominent creeping scale here argues against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sparing of the scrotum and penis with no satellite pustules

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Candidal intertrigo characteristically involves the scrotum and shows satellite papules/pustules with a beefy-red macerated surface; scrotal sparing and absent satellites strongly favour a dermatophyte over Candida.
Sparing of the scrotum/penis is typical of tinea cruris and is a key discriminator from candidal intertrigo.

symptoms.symptom_name: Explosive inflammatory flare (raw, burning, more extensive, less typically scaly) after 4 days of a potent topical corticosteroid — tinea incognito

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A rash that worsens and spreads after potent topical corticosteroid application is the signature of tinea incognito (steroid-modified dermatophyte infection) and both confirms the fungal aetiology and directs steroid cessation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Steroid-induced central epidermal atrophy / shiny skin thinning within the plaque

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Shiny atrophic thinning within the plaque reflects potent topical corticosteroid effect and corroborates the tinea incognito picture; this is a clinician close-inspection/palpation finding, sealed as answer-key and not reliably obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pruritus and rash strongly aggravated by sweating, friction and tight synthetic occlusive clothing in a heavy manual labourer

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Heat, sweat, friction and occlusion are dominant risk and aggravating factors for tinea cruris and are the targets of the hygiene/behavioural advice.

symptoms.symptom_name: Background recurrent tinea including tinea pedis (athlete's foot) — autoinoculation reservoir

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Concurrent tinea pedis is a classic source of groin re-infection (foot-to-groin autoinoculation when dressing); its presence supports a dermatophyte aetiology and must be treated to prevent relapse.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, no rigors, no rapidly spreading hot induration beyond the rash, no fluctuance and no purulent foul discharge (secondary bacterial cellulitis screened negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: secondary bacterial cellulitis of the groin/thigh
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of systemic features, spreading hot induration, fluctuance and purulence argues strongly against a secondary bacterial cellulitis/abscess and against antibiotics; the patient can self-report these, so the screen is achievable via telehealth. Definitive exclusion of deep induration/fluctuance still needs clinician palpation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Skin-scraping potassium hydroxide (KOH) microscopy and dermatophyte culture

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: KOH microscopy and dermatophyte culture confirm the fungus and definitively distinguish tinea from candidiasis, psoriasis and erythrasma; not obtainable via telehealth and reserved for atypical or treatment-refractory cases.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin-scraping for KOH microscopy and dermatophyte culture (if atypical or fails to respond)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the dermatophyte and excludes candidiasis/psoriasis/erythrasma; reserved for diagnostic doubt or treatment failure, and requires in-person sampling — not a telehealth action while the clinical picture is classic.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Baseline liver function tests if a course of oral terbinafine is prescribed
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Oral terbinafine is metabolised hepatically; baseline (and, for longer courses, monitoring) LFTs are standard before systemic therapy — arranged by the prescribing GP, not a remote reflex.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Broad blood panel / inflammatory markers ordered remotely
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated for a localised, systemically-well, cellulitis-negative fungal rash; ordering bloods over telehealth here is unnecessary over-investigation.
